evals.prompt: Implement the cancelOrder() method on the Order aggregate. Stack: Scala 3. The method should only allow cancellation of Draft or Confirmed orders. Cancelled and Shipped orders cannot be cancelled. Produce an implementation-summary contract.
evals.expected_output: cancelOrder() returns Either[OrderError, (Order, OrderCancelled)] with no thrown exceptions, plus 4 unit tests covering happy path and all rejection cases.
expectations: No throw/exception in domain logic — errors as typed values only
Cancelled orders return Left(AlreadyProcessed) or similar
Shipped orders return Left(CannotCancelShipped) or similar
At least 4 unit tests: cancel Draft, cancel Confirmed, reject Cancelled, reject Shipped
Tests are pure — no mocks needed for aggregate methods
implementation-summary contract produced with all required fields

evals.prompt: Implement the Money value object. Stack: Rust. Money must be a newtype with private fields, validated construction via TryFrom, add() that returns Result with CurrencyMismatch error, and Currency as an enum (not String). No unwrap() or panic!() in non-test code. Produce an implementation-summary contract.
evals.expected_output: Money struct with private fields, TryFrom<(i64, Currency)> impl rejecting negatives, add() returning Result<Money, DomainError>, Currency enum with USD/EUR/GBP.
expectations: Money is a newtype struct with private fields (not pub)
Construction via TryFrom returning Result — rejects negative amounts
add() returns Result<Money, DomainError> — never panics
Currency is an enum (USD, EUR, GBP) — not a String
Getters for amount and currency (no direct field access)
implementation-summary contract produced with all required fields
